steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence states that contour and box modeling process only thousands of shapes, providing the basis for the claim that they are more economical.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect: contour and box modeling process only thousands of shapes, and because of that they are more economical than surface model methods, which process millions. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence is a conclusion drawn from the first.
  • Choice A — Instead,: signals contrast or replacement, so fails — the second sentence draws a conclusion from the first, not an alternative to it. ✗
  • Choice B — Specifically,: signals a narrowing elaboration, so fails — the economy claim is a broader consequence, not a more detailed restatement of the shape count. ✗
  • Choice C — As such,: signals a logical result or conclusion, fitting perfectly — because they process fewer shapes, they are more economical. ✓
  • Choice D — For example,: signals an illustrative instance, so fails — the economy claim is a consequence, not an example of processing thousands of shapes. ✗
That matches Choice C — As such,.
